paper_title: Bayesian Analysis of Calibrated 14C Dates from Domasław

methods.description: This method involves using Bayesian analysis to refine the chronological information obtained from 14C dating of samples. By incorporating additional chronological information independent of the 14C dates, a more accurate chronology can be established.

steps.description: The 14C dates of individual samples were calibrated according to the IntCal13 curve using the OxCal v4.2.3 program.
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate 14C dates using IntCal13 curve with OxCal v4.2.3

steps.description: The relative dating results were used as a priori information in the Bayesian analysis of the whole set of dates.
steps.searchable_summary: Use relative dating results as a priori information in Bayesian analysis

steps.description: In the first approach (Model A), it was assumed that graves with transitional features come from phases absolutely separating the basic periods.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate Model A with transitional features assumption

steps.description: The internal coherence of Model A was assessed using the agreement index A.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess Model A coherence using agreement index

steps.description: In the revised model (Model B), it was assumed that graves with equipment exhibiting features of two consecutive periods can be contemporaneous with graves from one or the other period.
steps.searchable_summary: Revise model assumptions for Model B

steps.description: Bayesian analysis of Model B gives a very good agreement index, confirming its validity.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate Model B with Bayesian analysis
